cpSection: ECT Level I Basic Technique Course 1.0

lessons.title: Probe Coil Arrangements
objectives: Identify probe (surface) coils, encircling coils, and inside (bobbin) coils
Match each coil arrangement to the part geometries it suits
Describe inspection coverage for each arrangement
cp105Topics: 1.0 Types of eddy current sensing elements

lessons.title: Modes of Operation: Absolute, Differential, and Hybrid
objectives: Differentiate absolute, differential, and hybrid coil operating modes
Explain the theory of operation of each mode
Predict signal behavior for gradual vs. abrupt changes in each mode

lessons.title: Hall Effect Sensors
objectives: Explain the theory of operation of Hall effect sensors
Compare coil-based and Hall element sensing systems
Identify applications where Hall sensors offer advantages
cp105Topics: 1.1.4.2 Differences between coil and Hall element systems

lessons.title: Applications, Advantages, and Limitations
objectives: List the principal applications of eddy current sensing elements: material properties, flaw detection, and geometrical features
Summarize the advantages of eddy current testing
State the limitations that constrain eddy current applications

lessons.title: Factors Affecting Choice of Sensing Elements
objectives: Select a sensing element based on part type, discontinuity type, and probable flaw location
Account for testing speed and required coverage when choosing a sensor
Justify a probe selection for a given inspection scenario
cp105Topics: 1.2 Factors affecting choice of sensing elements
1.2.1 Type of part to be inspected
1.2.2 Type of discontinuity to be detected
